question: [File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass a collateralWithdrawals array containing the same collateralIndex twice under the precondition that position.collateral[idx] == X and the two withdrawal amounts sum to X, causing the first withdrawCollateral call to leave newCollateral > 0 (bit stays set) and the second to drain to 0 (bit cleared), then immediately re-supply 0 assets at that index via a separate supplyCollateral call, leaving collateral[idx] == 0 with bit cleared while a subsequent supplyCollateral with assets > 0 re-sets the bit, violating the invariant that collateral[idx] != 0 <=> bit is set at any intermediate observable state? Proof idea: fuzz collateralWithdrawals with duplicate indices and assert collateralBitmap(id, onBehalf) == expected_bitmap after each step.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass a collateralWithdrawals array where collateralWithdrawals[i].assets == 0 for a collateral index where position.collateral[idx] == 0 and the bit is already cleared, under the precondition that debt > 0, causing withdrawCollateral to call isHealthy which iterates the bitmap and skips that index, while a stale bit at a different index (bit set, collateral == 0) causes the oracle to be called for a zero-balance collateral, adding 0 to maxDebt and potentially allowing a position that is actually unhealthy to pass the health check? Proof idea: set up a stale bit state and assert isHealthy returns the same value as isHealthyNoBitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies with assets == 0 for a collateral index where oldCollateral == 0, under the precondition that the taker has debt > 0 and no other collateral, causing supplyCollateral to not set the bit (since oldCollateral == 0 && assets > 0 is false), then the subsequent take increases debt, and isHealthy iterates an empty bitmap returning maxDebt == 0 < debt, causing SellerIsLiquidatable to revert, effectively freezing the bundler flow for that taker? Proof idea: unit test with assets=0 supply followed by take and assert revert reason.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies with duplicate collateralIndex entries where the first entry has assets > 0 (setting the bit) and the second entry has assets == 0 (no-op), under the precondition that MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER - 1 bits are already set, causing the first supply to set the 16th bit (countBits == 16, passes check), and the second supply to be a no-op, but the bitmap now has 16 bits set while the taker believes they have 17 active collaterals, causing the liquidation loop to iterate only 16 collaterals and skip the phantom 17th? Proof idea: fuzz collateralSupplies with duplicates and assert countBits(bitmap) == actual active collateral count.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralWithdrawals where collateralWithdrawals[0].collateralIndex is a valid index (< market.collateralParams.length) but collateralWithdrawals[0].assets == position.collateral[idx] (full withdrawal), followed by collateralWithdrawals[1].collateralIndex == same index and assets == 0, under the precondition that debt > 0 after partial repayment, causing the first withdrawCollateral to clear the bit and the second to call isHealthy with the bit cleared, potentially allowing the health check to undercount maxDebt if that collateral was the only one supporting the remaining debt? Proof idea: assert that after the sequence, isHealthy(market, id, onBehalf) == false when remaining debt > 0 and all collateral withdrawn.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass collateralWithdrawals referencing a collateralIndex that is valid for position.collateral (0-127) but >= market.collateralParams.length, under the precondition that the taker has collateral at that index from a different market with the same id, causing withdrawCollateral to revert with array out-of-bounds when accessing market.collateralParams[collateralIndex].token, effectively freezing the bundler and preventing legitimate collateral withdrawal? Proof idea: create a market with N collaterals and pass collateralIndex == N in withdrawals.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex is valid but the corresponding token in market.collateralParams[idx].token is a fee-on-transfer token, under the precondition that the bundler pulls assets X but only X - fee is transferred to Midnight, causing supplyCollateral to record collateral[idx] = X while only X - fee tokens are held, setting the bit for a collateral whose oracle-quoted value exceeds the actual token balance, allowing the borrower to borrow more than the actual collateral value supports? Proof idea: deploy a fee-on-transfer collateral token and assert solvency invariant after supply.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies with MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER entries all at distinct indices, under the precondition that the taker already has 1 bit set, causing the 16th supplyCollateral call to set the 17th bit and revert with TooManyActivatedCollaterals, but the first 15 supplies have already been executed and the transaction reverts entirely, leaving the bitmap unchanged? Proof idea: assert that a reverted supplyCollateral does not partially update the bitmap.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralWithdrawals with N entries all at the same collateralIndex where the sum of assets equals position.collateral[idx], under the precondition that debt > 0 and the position is near the health threshold, causing each intermediate withdrawCollateral call to check health with a partially reduced collateral balance but the bit still set, potentially allowing a withdrawal that would be blocked if done atomically? Proof idea: compare single-step vs multi-step withdrawal and assert health check consistency.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass collateralWithdrawals where the market used for withdrawals (takes[0].offer.market) differs from the market where the taker actually has collateral, under the precondition that the taker has authorized the bundler, causing withdrawCollateral to be called on the wrong market, potentially withdrawing collateral from a market where the taker has no debt (health check trivially passes since debt == 0) while leaving the taker's actual debt market with a corrupted collateral balance? Proof idea: set up two markets and assert collateral balances in both after the bundler call.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex is the same for all entries but with increasing assets, under the precondition that oldCollateral == 0 initially, causing the first supply to set the bit and subsequent supplies to add to collateral without re-checking the bit, then a take increases debt, and the health check correctly uses the bit to compute maxDebt for the total collateral? Proof idea: fuzz N duplicate-index supplies and assert bitmap has exactly 1 bit set for that index.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass assets == 0 (zero repayment) with referralFeePct == 0, under the precondition that debt > 0 and collateralWithdrawals contains a valid withdrawal, causing repay(market, 0, onBehalf, ...) to be a no-op (debt unchanged) and then withdrawCollateral to check health with the full debt, potentially revealing a health check bypass if the bitmap has a stale bit that inflates maxDebt? Proof idea: set up a stale bit and assert isHealthy returns false when it should.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].assets causes position.collateral[idx] to overflow uint128 (i.e., oldCollateral + assets > type(uint128).max), under the precondition that oldCollateral is near type(uint128).max, causing toUint128 to revert with CastOverflow, reverting the entire transaction and leaving the bitmap unchanged? Proof idea: fuzz assets near uint128 max and assert no partial state update.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass collateralWithdrawals in reverse order of collateralIndex (e.g., [idx=5, idx=3, idx=1]) under the precondition that the taker has collateral at all three indices and debt > 0, causing each withdrawCollateral call to check health with the bitmap reflecting the remaining collateral, and assert that the health check at each step is equivalent to isHealthyNoBitmap with the same remaining collateral? Proof idea: differential test between bitmap-based and bitmap-less health check after each withdrawal.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where the collateralIndex is valid but the token in market.collateralParams[idx].token is address(0), under the precondition that the market was created with a zero-address collateral token (if allowed), causing pullToken to call safeTransferFrom on address(0) which may succeed silently, setting the bit for a collateral with no actual token backing, allowing the borrower to borrow against phantom collateral? Proof idea: check if market creation allows zero-address collateral tokens.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralWithdrawals where collateralWithdrawals[i].collateralIndex is a valid index but the bit is NOT set in the bitmap (missing bit scenario), under the precondition that collateral[idx] > 0 due to a previous bitmap corruption, causing withdrawCollateral to reduce collateral[idx] to 0 and attempt to clearBit on an already-cleared bit, leaving the bitmap unchanged but collateral[idx] == 0, creating a state where the bitmap is consistent but the collateral was silently drained? Proof idea: construct a missing-bit state and assert withdrawCollateral behavior.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex >= 128, under the precondition that the market has fewer than 128 collateral params, causing market.collateralParams[collateralIndex] to revert with array out-of-bounds before the bitmap is modified, effectively preventing the bundler from being used with high collateral indices? Proof idea: pass collateralIndex == 128 and assert revert.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass an empty collateralWithdrawals array (length == 0) under the precondition that the taker has debt > 0 and collateral, causing the bundler to skip all collateral withdrawals after the takes, leaving the bitmap unchanged, and assert that the bitmap correctly reflects all active collaterals after the takes? Proof idea: assert bitmap is unchanged when collateralWithdrawals is empty.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where the same collateralIndex appears N times with assets > 0 each time, under the precondition that oldCollateral == 0 initially, causing the first supply to set the bit and N-1 subsequent supplies to add collateral without modifying the bitmap, then assert that countBits(bitmap) == 1 for that index regardless of N? Proof idea: fuzz N and assert bitmap bit count.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralWithdrawals where collateralWithdrawals[0].assets == position.collateral[idx] - 1 (partial withdrawal leaving 1 wei) and collateralWithdrawals[1].assets == 1 (draining the last wei), under the precondition that debt > 0 and the position is exactly at the health threshold, causing the first withdrawal to leave 1 wei (bit stays set, health check passes) and the second withdrawal to drain to 0 (bit cleared, health check passes with 0 collateral), potentially allowing a position to become undercollateralized? Proof idea: fuzz dust amounts and assert health invariant after each step.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex is valid but the permit data is malformed, under the precondition that the bundler uses ERC2612 permit, causing the permit call to fail silently (try/catch in pullToken) and then safeTransferFrom to revert due to insufficient allowance, reverting the entire transaction before any bitmap modification? Proof idea: pass invalid permit data and assert no bitmap change.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker pass collateralWithdrawals where collateralWithdrawals[i].assets is exactly position.collateral[idx] for each index, under the precondition that the taker has debt > 0 and multiple active collaterals, causing each withdrawCollateral to clear the corresponding bit and check health with the remaining collaterals, and assert that the final state has an empty bitmap and zero collateral balances? Proof idea: assert bitmap == 0 after withdrawing all collateral.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].collateralIndex is valid but the market passed to supplyCollateral differs from the market used for takes (due to the bundler using takes[0].offer.market for both), under the precondition that the taker has authorized the bundler, causing the collateral to be supplied to the correct market but the health check in take to use the bitmap for the same market, and assert that the bitmap is consistent? Proof idea: verify market consistency between supply and take operations.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralWithdrawals where the collateralIndex is valid but the oracle for that collateral reverts, under the precondition that debt > 0 after partial repayment, causing withdrawCollateral to call isHealthy which calls the oracle and reverts, preventing the withdrawal and freezing the collateral? Proof idea: deploy a reverting oracle and assert withdrawCollateral reverts.

[File: 'src/periphery/MidnightBundles.sol -> Scope: High Collateral bitmap corruption causing health checks or liquidation loops to skip active collateral or include inactive collateral'] [Function: supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged borrower pass collateralSupplies where collateralSupplies[i].assets is 1 wei for a collateral index where oldCollateral == 0, under the precondition that the market has 16 collateral params and the taker already has 15 bits set, causing the 16th supply to set the 16th bit (countBits == 16, passes TooManyActivatedCollaterals check), and then a subsequent supply at a 17th index to revert, and assert that the bitmap has exactly 16 bits set? Proof idea: fuzz collateral supply sequences near the MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER limit.
